status_notes: Initial research from AWS API Gateway documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live AWS environment).
notes: Managed API service (REST, HTTP, WebSocket). Security includes authentication, authorization, throttling, WAF integration, TLS.

data.description: Use AWS IAM policies and Signature Version 4 (Sigv4) signed requests to control access to API methods. Callers must have execute-api:Invoke permission. Supports IAM users, roles, and cross-account access via resource policies.

options.description: Set on each method to require IAM authentication
Requests must be signed with valid AWS credentials

data.vendor_recommendation: Use IAM authorization for service-to-service API calls where callers already have AWS credentials. Combine with resource policies for cross-account access.

audit.note: Check authorizationType field. Value should be AWS_IAM for IAM-authorized methods.
audit.console: API Gateway → APIs → {API} → Resources → {Method} → Method Request

match_nodes.description: Amazon Cognito user pool authorization for API methods

data.description: Use Amazon Cognito user pools as an authorizer for API methods. Clients authenticate with Cognito, receive a JWT token, and pass it in the Authorization header. API Gateway validates the token against the specified user pool.

options.description: Validates JWT tokens from a Cognito user pool
HTTP header containing the identity or access token
OAuth 2.0 scopes to require on the access token

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Cognito authorizers for user-facing APIs where you need sign-up, sign-in, and token management. Configure authorization scopes for fine-grained access control.

audit.note: Check for authorizers with type COGNITO_USER_POOLS.

match_nodes.description: Custom authorization logic via Lambda function

data.description: Use an AWS Lambda function to implement custom authorization logic. The Lambda function receives the caller's identity (token or request parameters) and returns an IAM policy document that determines access. Supports token-based (JWT, OAuth) and request-based (headers, query strings, stage variables) authorizers.

options.description: Lambda receives the token from a specified header. Supports token validation caching.
Lambda receives the full request context (headers, query strings, path parameters, stage variables).

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Lambda authorizers for custom auth schemes (third-party tokens, SAML, custom logic). Enable authorization caching to reduce Lambda invocations and latency. Set appropriate TTL based on token expiry.

audit.note: Check for authorizers with type TOKEN or REQUEST.

configure.cli: aws apigateway create-authorizer --rest-api-id {api-id} --name MyLambdaAuth --type TOKEN --authorizer-uri arn:aws:apigateway:{region}:lambda:path/2015-03-31/functions/{lambda-arn}/invocations --identity-source method.request.header.Authorization

match_nodes.description: Usage plan API keys for throttling and metering

data.description: Alphanumeric string tokens distributed to API consumers for usage tracking and throttling via usage plans. API keys identify the caller and associate them with a usage plan that defines throttle and quota limits. API keys are NOT a security mechanism by themselves.

options.description: Associates API keys with throttle rate, burst limit, and quota
Where to look for the API key

default.note: Methods do not require API keys unless apiKeyRequired is set to true.

data.vendor_recommendation: API keys are for throttling, not security. Always use API keys together with an authorizer (IAM, Cognito, or Lambda) for actual authentication and authorization.

audit.console: API Gateway → API Keys; API Gateway → Usage Plans

data.description: Mutual TLS authentication requires clients to present a valid X.509 certificate. API Gateway validates the client certificate against a truststore (PEM file in S3). Requires a custom domain name. Available for REST and HTTP APIs.

options.description: S3 URI pointing to the PEM file containing trusted CA certificates
S3 object version for the truststore file

data.vendor_recommendation: Use mutual TLS for high-security API access, especially for B2B integrations. Combine with other authorization mechanisms for defense in depth. Requires a custom domain name — cannot use the default execute-api endpoint.

data.description: JSON resource policies attached to an API that control whether a specified principal (AWS account, IAM user/role, IP address, VPC endpoint) can invoke the API. Similar to S3 bucket policies. Evaluated together with IAM policies for the final authorization decision.

options.description: AWS account, IAM entity, or * (with conditions)
Restrict by IP address, VPC endpoint, or VPC

data.vendor_recommendation: Use resource policies to restrict API access by IP range or VPC endpoint. Essential for private APIs (accessible only from within a VPC). Combine with IAM or Lambda authorizers for layered security.

audit.note: Check the policy field in the response.

match_nodes.description: Rate limiting at account, stage, and method levels

data.description: API Gateway throttles requests to protect backend services. Throttling is applied at multiple levels: account-level (shared across all APIs), stage-level (per deployment stage), and method-level (per resource and HTTP method). Uses token bucket algorithm.

options.description: Shared limit across all APIs in a region
Override account defaults for a specific stage
Fine-grained throttle per resource and HTTP method
Per-client throttle via API keys and usage plans

default.note: Account-level defaults. Can be increased via service quota request.

data.vendor_recommendation: Configure method-level throttling for expensive or sensitive endpoints. Use usage plans for per-client rate limiting. Monitor with CloudWatch 429 error metrics.

audit.note: Check methodSettings for per-method throttling configuration.

match_nodes.description: Attach AWS WAF web ACL to API stage

data.description: Associate an AWS WAF web ACL with an API Gateway stage to filter requests at the API level. WAF rules are evaluated before authorization. Protects against common web exploits (SQLi, XSS), bot traffic, and allows IP-based filtering.
data.vendor_recommendation: Enable WAF on all public-facing APIs. Use AWS managed rule groups (Core Rule Set, SQL injection, Known Bad Inputs) as a baseline. Add custom rules for application-specific protection.

audit.note: If no web ACL is associated, the command returns an error.
audit.console: API Gateway → APIs → {API} → Stages → {Stage} → Web ACL
